item.title: Oaktree’s Howard Marks Says Investors Are Underestimating AI
item.description: Artificial intelligence is making the world more unpredictable than ever before, according to Oaktree Capital Management co-founder Howard Marks.

item.title: Asian Stocks Poised for Gains in Cautious Trading: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian equities were primed for a positive open Wednesday after US stocks and Treasuries edged higher, signaling that investors are attempting to look through near-term geopolitical uncertainty.

item.title: Gold Steady Near $5,000 as Investors Weigh Fed Rate-Cut Path
item.description: Gold traded in a narrow range as investors weighed the Federal Reserve’s rate-cut path against inflationary risks from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: SEC Chairman Floats Scaling Company Reporting to Firm Size
item.description: The head of the Securities and Exchange Commission floated scaling the frequency of corporate disclosures to a firm’s size, as the regulator weighs changes to earnings report requirements.

item.title: Prudential Business Profit Gains, Adds $1.2 Billion Buyback
item.description: Prudential Plc’s new business profit rose, thanks to growth in Hong Kong and China markets, as the insurer said it will buy back an additional $1.2 billion in its own stock.

item.title: Crude Oil Holds Advance as Iran Confirms Death of Security Chief
item.description: Oil largely held a gain as the war in the Middle East dragged on, with Iran confirming the death of its security chief in a further intensification of the conflict that’s roiled global energy markets.

item.title: Apple’s Head of Home Hardware Leaves for Smart Ring Maker Oura
item.description: Apple Inc.’s top hardware engineering executive overseeing home devices is leaving for smart ring maker Oura Health Oy, marking a setback for a unit already contending with product delays.

item.title: Korea Seeks Fuel From Shareholder Meets After 138% Gain Stalls
item.description: South Korea’s corporate reform drive faces a crucial test as shareholder meeting season enters full swing, with investors demanding concrete steps to revive a world-beating stock rally.

item.title: Ali Larijani, Key Wartime Leader in Iran, Is Killed at 67
item.description: Ali Larijani, a veteran politician who helped lead Iran’s wartime efforts against the US and Israel, was killed in an Israeli airstrike, Iranian state media confirmed. He was 67.

item.title: BHP Names Craig as CEO as Mining Giant Focuses on Growth
item.description: BHP Group appointed Brandon Craig as its new chief executive officer, as the world’s biggest miner seeks to embark on a new leg of growth in copper and potash, while managing a slowdown in iron ore.

item.title: Traders Dial Back Bets Against Fed Cuts as Growth Worries Build
item.description: Bond traders are scaling back some of the aggressive bets that had largely driven markets to price out Federal Reserve interest rate cuts this year.

item.title: Iran Confirms Death of Security Chief Larijani: Tasnim
item.description: Iranian state media confirmed the death of Ali Larijani, the secretary of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council and a key pillar of the country’s wartime leadership.

item.title: Record Aussie Bond Sales Boom Dented by Iran War, Rising Yields
item.description: Australia’s market for bond sales is trailing US and European peers since the start of the Iran war, as the conflict stokes inflation and pushes local borrowing costs to some of the highest in the developed world.

item.title: BOJ to Sit Tight With Oil Surge Set to Fuel Inflation
item.description: The Bank of Japan is widely expected to keep its benchmark rate unchanged on Thursday as soaring oil prices are set to complicate efforts for the central bank to reach its 2% stable inflation target.

item.title: War in Iran Sends Energy Shocks Rippling Through Asia
item.description: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast we look at how the global energy crunch sparked by the war in Iran is pushing up prices and forcing Asian countries to take drastic action.

item.title: Haven Dollar Makes Comeback as a Hedge to the Stock Market
item.description: The dollar and US stocks are at their most inversely correlated in nearly a year, reasserting the typical relationship between the assets associated with the greenback’s haven status.

item.title: Itausa Sees Aegea Valuation Topping $7.8 Billion in Brazil IPO
item.description: Aegea Saneamento e Participacoes SA shareholder Itausa SA said it expects the sanitation company to fetch a valuation above its current 40.5 billion reais ($7.8 billion) as it prepares for an initial public offering in coming months.

item.title: Trump’s Oil Reserve Release Is Reshaping the Futures Curve
item.description: As oil traders brace for a historic release from the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve in the form of a loan, they’re dumping prompt barrels and snapping up cheaper supplies further out when borrowers will need to repay the government.

item.title: Meta to Discontinue Key Metaverse Product For VR Headsets
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. said that users of its Quest headsets will lose access to Horizon Worlds, a virtual destination where cartoon versions of people can meet up and play games, marking the latest pullback from a strategy once central to Mark Zuckerberg’s vision of a so-called metaverse.

item.title: Nvidia CEO Says Company Is Firing Up H200 Production for China
item.description: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang said the company is firing up manufacturing of H200 AI accelerators for customers in China, a sign of progress in the chipmaker’s effort to reenter the vital market.

item.title: Barclays Says Soaring BDC Risk Premiums Are ‘Justified’
item.description: Investors are demanding higher risk premiums to own the debt of business development companies, reflecting their anxiety around private credit exposure, according to Barclays Plc.

item.title: AI Drone Software Stock Jumps 700% in Best IPO Since Newsmax
item.description: Swarmer Inc. shares skyrocketed 700% on Tuesday, putting the artificial intelligence drone software maker on pace for the best trading debut by a US stock since Newsmax Inc.’s blockbuster entry nearly a year ago.

item.title: Canadian Dollar’s Resilience at Risk as Central Bankers Meet
item.description: One of the most resilient currencies of early March — the Canadian dollar — is at risk of losing its edge as the nation’s central bankers gather this week to assess its weakened economy.

item.title: Barclays’ Altmann Sees Strongest Buy Signal for Stocks in a Year
item.description: US stocks are flashing their strongest buy signal in almost a year, according to Barclays’ Alex Altmann, who joins a growing chorus on Wall Street saying the worst of the recent rout may be over.

item.title: Trump Again Vows to Block Wind Turbines During His Presidency
item.description: US President Donald Trump said he wanted to have no wind turbines built during his presidency, reiterating his distaste for the renewable energy source after his administration has made multiple moves to thwart its development.

item.title: JPMorgan Halts Qualtrics $5.3 Billion Debt Deal on Software Pain
item.description: A group of banks led by JPMorgan Chase & Co. halted a $5.3 billion debt deal for software firm Qualtrics International Inc. after failing to win over investors amid deepening anxiety surrounding artificial intelligence disruption.

item.title: Spain Weighs Buying More Algerian Gas as War Tightens Supplies
item.description: Spain is considering buying more natural gas via pipeline from Algeria to help boost supplies as the war in the Middle East is causing a surge in prices.

item.title: Alcoa Draws New Aluminum Orders as Gulf States Curb Production
item.description: Alcoa Corp., the largest US aluminum producer, says it’s fielding interest from buyers seeking alternative supply following the curtailment of production in the Middle East.

item.title: Redwood Capital Among Creditors to Get Control of Bankrupt REIT
item.description: Office Properties Income Trust, the bankrupt owner of more than 120 office buildings, is nearing a deal to settle a months-long restructuring case by handing ownership to creditors in exchange for cutting its debt load by $700 million to about $1.7 billion.

item.title: EV Startup Olinia Powers Up in Mexico Thanks to State Support
item.description: Mexico’s government-backed electric vehicle startup Olinia will unveil its first two prototypes in June as it seeks $200 million in private capital to jumpstart manufacturing, all part of an ambitious plan to transform how people move around cities.
